title: Data/corpus poisoning & secret-leak indicator detector
intent: DETECT data/corpus poisoning indicators in INGESTED training/corpus samples — backdoor trigger tokens, label-flip markers, anomalous repeated trigger phrases, hidden unicode triggers, and secret/credential leakage into uploads. Output is a verdict (data) only; matched content is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces SISAI's verified-only corpus feedback + fingerprint/provenance doctrine (docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md).

patterns.desc: Explicit backdoor/trigger-token framing announcing a poisoned activation phrase planted in a sample.
Label-flip / mislabel marker that forces a corpus entry to a target class regardless of content.
Conspicuous magic activation marker / sentinel token typical of backdoor triggers (e.g. cf-trigger, TRIGGER_, [[activate]], delimiter sentinels).
Anomalous high-frequency repetition of the same rare token/phrase — a statistical hallmark of trigger-sample stuffing.
Hidden zero-width / bidi control characters embedded in a sample to encode an invisible trigger.
Concrete credential / secret material leaked into an ingested/upload sample (API keys, private keys, tokens, connection strings).
Secret-bearing key/value assignment carrying a real-looking high-entropy value smuggled into a corpus sample.
Sample instructing that ingested data / secrets be uploaded to an external LLM or sink — secret-leak via external-assistant upload.

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. A matched sample is quarantined and BLOCKED from corpus feedback until provenance review (verified-only corpus doctrine). False-positive policy: normal training text and third-person descriptions of poisoning must NOT match (see verify suite).
